# RAM_ISTP — Prioritized Improvement Plan (synthesis of adversarially verified review, 2026-07-02)

## 1. Executive verdict

The core engine is in good shape: 266 tests green, the signals-only/firewall ethos is genuinely intact in the Rust code (zero order-placing paths, verified), and the worst prior operational bug (the 93-second wedged cache) is already fixed and self-healing. **The weakest theme is that the app's own honesty machinery doesn't reach the surfaces you actually trade from**: /picks skips the DSR multiple-testing gate the scanner already has, the desk stages brackets with the wrong stop geometry and no tradability filter, and the safety features built this week (governor, exit guard, cost floor) are live JSON with no UI. The second theme is silent ops failure: the nightly pipeline can skip, stall for 6 hours, or half-fail while still reporting SUCCESS, and nothing on any page tells you the data is a session old. Nothing here requires re-architecting — almost everything is a few hours of wiring existing, already-correct data to the right place.

---

## 2. Fix this week (P0 + quick-win P1s)

**W1. Port the DSR gate into the /picks / Finder path — P0, hours.**
*Why it matters:* your primary morning list currently ranks names by a Confidence score that never deflates for picking the best of 120 configs per symbol — a name your own deep-dive would cap at "Low confidence (selection artifact)" can sit at the top of the buy list. This is the exact bug already fixed in the scanner (the comment at `src/suggestion_engine.rs:1155-1159` describes it verbatim) and never ported to `fit_symbol` (`:1428-1446`, `dsr: 1.0` at `:458`). Implementer note: parity fix is trial_sharpes + `deflated_sharpe` only (~15 lines); gate_and_pick does not fill robustness_pct, so don't over-port. Also correct the false "gate-passed" docstring at `src/picks.rs:4`.

**W2. Stage desk brackets from the row's own SL/target, not the globals — P1, hours.**
*Why:* the desk's paste-ready order text can risk **1.2x–2.0x** your selected tier risk at 5x MIS leverage — qty is sized for a 0.75×ATR stop but staged with a 1.5×ATR stop. `src/server.rs:1017-1020` discards the correct `row.sl`/`row.target` that `FinderRow` already carries. Derive the ATR-mults from the row (or add `stage_signal_abs`); add one test asserting staged loss-at-stop ≈ capital × tier risk.

**W3. Add the /picks tradability + cost-floor filter to /api/staging — P1, hours.**
*Why:* the desk can currently stage a T2T/GSM-blocked name (whose MIS order will be rejected — or worse, that you can't exit intraday) or a name whose edge is smaller than its own costs. Reuse the exact filter /picks already has (`src/picks.rs:109-115`) in `staging_handler`, plus a `tradability_note` field and `tradability_verified=false` on cold cache.

**W4. Honest X/10 on /picks: Wilson floor + show n — P1, hours.**
*Why:* the one number you act on each morning rounds an in-sample, best-of-120-configs win% **up** (65.9% over 59 trades shows "7/10"; the honest floor is ~6/10) and hides the sample size entirely. Compute X/10 from `wilson_lower` (`src/stats.rs:141`, already used by the scanner), render `n=NN` under each row (the field is already in the JSON, `picks.rs:44`), amber-flag n<30. *(Merges two confirmed findings — same file, one change.)*

**W5. Stale-data day becomes visible + self-healing — P1, hours.** Two joined fixes:
- **Scheduler:** `scheduled_refresh.py:284-291` writes the SUCCESS marker even when the Kite download was skipped/failed (`daily_update.sh` cannot exit nonzero) — a forgotten morning login means you trade all day on yesterday's edges with a log that says "SUCCESS". Pre-check `.kite_token.json` date==today; on skip, log loudly, notify, and **don't write the marker** so the evening login self-heals it.
- **UI:** add `data_session: "YYYY-MM-DD"` to `SignalPacket` and `PicksResult`; red/amber banner on `/`, `/picks`, `/live_trade_plan` whenever data_session != today (IST); rename the footer "last update" (which shows wall-clock even in replay, `main.rs:657`) to "rendered at".
*Why:* this closes the single most likely way you unknowingly trade a whole session on stale signals.

**W6. macOS failure notifications + let failures actually fail — P1, hours.**
*Why:* a failed, skipped, or killed nightly run is currently invisible unless you read launchd logs — it has bitten twice this week. Add an `osascript` notify helper to `scheduled_refresh.py` (fire on no-token skip, nonzero rc, and on SUCCESS with the session date as positive confirmation), and make `daily_update.sh` propagate a nonzero exit for stage-2/3 failures so the existing retry machinery stops being dead code.

**W7. Shard the scheduled stage-3 download 8 ways — P1, hours.**
*Why:* the nightly refresh currently takes ~3.5–6 h single-threaded (both of the last two scheduled runs needed manual rescue) while your proven 8-shard path finishes in ~5 min — and you've said "always parallel only". Replace `daily_update.sh:42` with the 8-shard loop, collect per-shard rc (feeds W6), write per-shard log files, update the stale rate-limit comment in `03_download_1min_zerodha.py:205-207`.

**W8. Credential hygiene sweep (one sitting, ~1 hour) — P1.**
*Why:* your long-lived broker secret has plaintext copies one `git add -A` or one grep away from leaking, and one key is already treated as compromised. In order:
1. **Rotate INDIANAPI_KEY** (pending since Jun-30) and the **Kite API secret** (8 copies in `~/.zsh_history`, 6 in `~/.zsh_sessions`).
2. Add `.env.*` + `!.env.example` to `.gitignore`; **delete `.env.bak.1782915514`** (it holds an uncommented token line + all keys, is un-ignored, and the GitHub remote is public).
3. Scrub `KITE_API_SECRET=`/`KITE_API_KEY=`/`INDIANAPI_KEY=` lines from zsh history/sessions (use `LC_ALL=C grep -a` — zsh metafies history), then restart open shells.
4. Flip token precedence in `03_download_1min_zerodha.py:103-106` (today's `.kite_token.json` before env), drop `KITE_ACCESS_TOKEN` from `CRED_KEYS` in `scheduled_refresh.py:39`, fix the two "export KITE_ACCESS_TOKEN" help strings — the Jul-1 two-hour outage is otherwise one uncomment away from recurring. Change `start.sh`/`run_all.sh` to `set -a; source ../.env; set +a` so secrets are never typed again.
5. Bonus (5 min): add a `deny` block for `mcp__kite__place_order/modify_order/cancel_order/*gtt*` to `.claude/settings.json` — extends the app's "never places orders" guarantee to the AI tooling that works in this repo.

**W9. Stop re-scanning the universe every 3 minutes all session — P1, hours.**
*Why:* the scheduler re-runs the full 1,768-symbol backtest ~130×/day for byte-identical results (data only changes at the 16:00 refresh) — ~35-40 min of scanning and ~3 GB of parquet churn per pass on the exact swap-starved box where memory pressure made cold scans 9x slower. Date-key the scanner result the way `fit_universe` already is (`suggestion_engine.rs:1479-1484`), or raise the TTL to hours and invalidate on data-refresh completion. Same treatment for swing/regime.

**W10. Session-governor banner on the three decision pages — P1, hours.**
*Why:* the tilt kill-switch you asked for exists only as JSON — after 3 straight losses the app looks identical to 9:15 AM. One shared ~30-line snippet fetching `/api/session_governor` every 60 s on index/picks/live_trade_plan: nothing when open, amber strip on caution, full-width red STOP on closed. Display-only, firewall-consistent.

---

## 3. Fix this month (remaining P1s)

**M1. Show the honest denominator on every "eligible edges" surface — day.**
*Why:* 99.1% of "eligible" edges (1,611/1,625 in the live 30min map) fail the app's own DSR artifact threshold, and 22% have negative OOS — yet style validation, calibration, edge-map counts, and /add_stock quote raw eligible counts. Add a second tier everywhere: "N eligible / M pass DSR≥0.5 & OOS>0", and have style_validation's verdict lead with the smaller number (its "35/1318 small-caps" headline is ~98% artifacts). `eligible()` itself stays untouched (anchor-safe). Do this **before** style_validation/calibration get UI panels.

**M2. Run the 2× slippage stress in the finder and drop picks that die at it — days.**
*Why:* the backtest fills at the exact price that triggered the signal with a flat 2.5bp slippage, but /picks concentrates in thin small-caps where real slippage is 10-100bp — and the displayed edges (+0.1–0.3R) are the same order as the cost model's error bar. The 2×/3× stress already exists in the deep-dive but never reaches the finder. Implementer shortcut (verified): exit decisions are cost-independent, so the 2× stress is a linear per-trade cost shift in `fit_symbol` — no refill needed. (Per-symbol spread-derived slippage is **not** implementable from the tradability cache as once suggested — it has no spread data.)

**M3. Make `backtest 1day` refuse loudly; build a real multi-day simulator before the swing page — day.**
*Why:* the 1day edge map is empty **by construction** (the simulator skips any entry whose next bar is a different day — on daily bars that's every entry, `strategy_engine.rs:752-754`), not because daily edges don't exist — the handover's own root-cause note is wrong. The planned weekend swing page's "20-day daily-breakout edge" structurally cannot exist in this map. Short-term: hard error with an explicit message instead of silently writing `[]`. For swing: a separate, clearly-labeled multi-day-hold fill mode first.

**M4. Exit-guard verdict + cost-floor line on /live_trade_plan — day.**
*Why:* the two pre-trade safety checks built this week never appear at the moment of decision — the page shows a fully sized Entry/SL/Target/Qty with no "your stop may not fill" or "cost floor exceeds the edge" even when `cost_stand_aside=true` (the block verdict doesn't consult it). (1) Call `/api/exit_guard` with the plan's numbers and render its verdict in the verdict box; (2) render `break_even_r` vs `expectancy_r` from data already in the JSON, forcing STAND ASIDE styling when flagged.

**M5. A guided "Today" strip on the home page — day.**
*Why:* the morning loop (kite → picks → plan each name → board) exists only in the handover; the home page has 10 tabs, **no link to /picks anywhere**, and the Kite success message routes you away from trading (to /data). Add a 4-chip strip driven by existing endpoints: ① Kite login ✓/✗ ② Get picks ③ Plan each name ④ Governor status. Fix kite.html's next-step link. Fold in the trivial P2: add Picks to every page's nav and unify picks.html's divergent mini-nav.

---

## 4. Later / nice-to-have (P2s)

**Verified P2s:**
- **Cache hardening (RAII drop-guard + fallback store)** — the single-flight guard still leaks if compute *panics* in `maybe_refresh`/startup-warm threads (the dcea92f follow-up), and the 90s-loser fallback never stores its result or re-claims the guard, so a wedged cold slot never heals and concurrent waiters stampede duplicate scans. One `RefreshGuard` type (~30-40 lines) + `cache.store()` in the fallback closes the whole class. Low-probability trigger, but cheap and already designed.
- **Backups** — no Time Machine destination, journal DuckDB lives mostly in its WAL, `.env` has no off-machine copy. Loss risk is smaller than it looks (daily CSV journal export exists; journal is currently 10 re-importable broker rows), but an rsync step after the nightly + configuring TM (excluding the 60 GB archive) is an hour well spent.
- **Corp-actions write-aside-then-swap** — stage 5 deletes all corp-action parquets *before* re-downloading with `|| true`; a throttled run silently rebuilds `_adj` archives from a shrunken action set. Blast radius verified small (nothing in the decision path reads `_adj`; the data-quality caption errs honest), but the ~15-line atomic swap is correct hygiene.
- **Scanner/finder shared pass** — the identical heavy loop runs twice on cold builds; a shared per-symbol pass (emitting raw ConfigStats so each consumer applies its own gate — important for the anchors) halves cold-start cost.
- **DuckDB memory limit** — the 2 GB/connection "backstop" is a 22 GB ceiling on an 18 GB box; drop to 512 MB. Note: `.env` line 12 pins it, so edit `.env`, not just the code default.

**Unverified P2 suggestions (plausible, not adversarially confirmed — spot-check before acting):**
- *Correctness:* direct unit tests on `find_capital_fit`/`size_scan_result` (the sizing math is only tested by a copy of itself — the W2 bug shipped through exactly this gap; probably the highest-value item in this section); single-flight + data-fingerprint version key for `FIT_UNIVERSE_CACHE` (currently `date|count`, so a manual refresh without restart serves stale prices); recover poisoned journal mutex (`into_inner()`) so one panic can't silently kill the daily-loss circuit breaker.
- *Statistical honesty:* label /picks numbers "in-sample" and eventually deflate across the top-5-of-1700 cross-sectional selection; calibration's `Imported` fallback should use the *median* eligible edge, not the max, or the scorecard will converge to "engine over-confident" for the wrong reason; show "prices as of last session close" on pick cards (entry = yesterday's close) or re-anchor to live LTP.
- *Ops/logging:* add timing/log lines to `maybe_refresh` (scheduler scans are currently invisible — 0 log lines from ~130 daily scans); replace the `tee` process substitution in `daily_update.sh` with plain append redirection; log swap usage before heavy stages; re-run `backtest 1day` after M3 and add it to the nightly TFS list once it produces edges.
- *UX:* symbol handoff links picks → `/live_trade_plan?symbol=…&capital=…&risk=…` (kills morning re-typing under time pressure); a "Your style vs the evidence" card on desk.html for `/api/style_validation` (after M1's two-tier counts land).
- *Security:* Host-header middleware on 127.0.0.1:8787 (DNS-rebinding/CSRF can currently wipe the journal or overwrite the stored Kite secret via unauthenticated POSTs — arguably promote this one); fix the `.gitignore` pattern to match dot-prefixed `.kite_token.json`; delete the `print("ACCESS_TOKEN = …")` at `03_download_1min_zerodha.py:97`.
- *Performance:* compact (non-pretty) edge-map JSON + an eligible-only sidecar for startup; stagger the 5 startup-warm threads; shared connection pool for request-path endpoints.

---

## 5. Deliberately NOT recommended

- **Any order placement or modification** — by the app *or* by AI sessions via the Kite MCP. The right move is the opposite: deny-rules (W8.5). Signals only, always.
- **Loosening the DSR/eligibility gates or "smoothing" the X/10 so more picks appear.** W1/W4/M1 will make lists shorter and scores lower — that is the system working. Padding them back up would be fabrication.
- **Feeding tradability, news, governor, exit-guard, or style-validation into the scoring engine.** They stay display-only/firewalled; every fix above wires them to your *eyes*, never to Confidence.
- **Shipping the swing page on the current 1day edge map** or back-filling it with the setup-only swing_analyzer as if it were a backtested edge — there is no ground truth for multi-day holds until a labeled multi-day simulator exists (M3).
- **Intraday data auto-refresh to make /picks feel "live".** Intraday archive data is static by design; the honest fix is labeling the as-of session (W5), not faking freshness.
- **Hiding the "unavailable"/caveat states** (tradability_verified=false, calibration n=4, honest-empty maps). Honest "unavailable" beats fabricated confidence — that's the product.

---

## 6. Summary table

| # | Item | Severity | Effort | Trading impact |
|---|------|----------|--------|----------------|
| W1 | DSR gate in /picks/finder path | P0 | hours | Morning top-5 stops ranking overfit selection artifacts |
| W2 | Desk brackets use row's own SL/target | P1 | hours | Staged stop stops silently risking 1.2–2.0x tier risk at 5x leverage |
| W3 | Tradability + cost-floor filter on /api/staging | P1 | hours | Desk stops staging un-exitable / negative-net-edge names |
| W4 | Wilson-floor X/10 + show n on /picks | P1 | hours | The number you act on stops overstating by ~1–1.5/10 on small-n names |
| W5 | Stale-session marker fix + data-date banners | P1 | hours | Can no longer trade a whole day on yesterday's edges unknowingly |
| W6 | Failure notifications + real exit codes | P1 | hours | Failed/skipped/killed nightly runs become visible, retries work |
| W7 | 8-shard scheduled download | P1 | hours | Nightly refresh ~16:15 instead of ~22:00; no more manual rescues |
| W8 | Credential sweep (rotate, gitignore, scrub, token precedence, MCP deny) | P1 | hours | Closes leak-to-GitHub path, the Jul-1 outage mode, and AI-order risk |
| W9 | Date-key the scanner (stop 130 redundant scans/day) | P1 | hours | Removes the biggest self-inflicted load during live-session hours |
| W10 | Governor banner on decision pages | P1 | hours | Tilt kill-switch actually interrupts you mid-session |
| M1 | Two-tier eligible counts (DSR+OOS survivors) | P1 | day | Style/calibration verdicts stop quoting ~98%-artifact headline stats |
| M2 | 2× slippage stress reaches the finder | P1 | days | Thin-name picks that die at realistic slippage stop showing as tradeable |
| M3 | 1day backtest refuses; real swing simulator first | P1 | day | Weekend swing feature won't ship on a structurally empty evidence base |
| M4 | Exit-guard + cost-floor on live_trade_plan | P1 | day | "Stop may not fill" / "costs exceed edge" visible at the last checkpoint |
| M5 | "Today" workflow strip + Picks in nav | P1 | day | The morning routine is on-screen instead of in a handover doc |
| L1 | Cache RAII guard + fallback store | P2 | day | No panic can ever wedge a cache slot again |
| L2 | Journal/.env backups + Time Machine | P2 | hours | Trading record survives disk failure |
| L3 | Corp-actions atomic swap | P2 | day | Network hiccup can't silently rewrite adjusted history |
| L4 | Shared scanner/finder pass | P2 | day | Cold builds cost one universe scan, not two |
| L5 | DuckDB memory limit 512MB (.env) | P2 | hours | OOM backstop actually backstops on the 18 GB box |
| L6 | Unverified batch (sizing tests, Host-header auth, picks→plan links, logging, etc.) | P2 | varies | Regression net, drive-by-web protection, less morning friction |

Suggested implementation order: W1 → W2 → W3 (same code area, one sitting) → W8 (independent, do today) → W5+W6+W7 (one ops sitting) → W4 → W9 → W10 → then the month list. Re-run the full suite + both anchors after W1/W2/M2 — they touch scoring-adjacent code.
